title: Coastal Monitoring
text: Coastal monitoring combines field observations, satellite imagery, and spatial analysis to understand how shorelines change over time.

title: Marine Biodiversity
text: Marine biodiversity studies describe how species interact with habitats, environmental gradients, and human pressure.

title: Clean Code
text: Readable code helps future collaborators understand the purpose of a function, the structure of the data, and the expected result.

title: Musical Phrasing
text: Musical phrasing is the way a player shapes notes, silences, accents, and breathing to create meaning.

title: Reading an Abstract
text: An abstract usually presents the research problem, the methods, the main findings, and the conclusion in a compact form.

title: Working With Maps
text: A good map uses clear layers, readable labels, a consistent coordinate system, and a legend that supports interpretation.

title: Database Thinking
text: A database stores information in a structured way so that queries can retrieve, filter, compare, and update records.

title: Listening Through Music
text: Music lessons in English train vocabulary, rhythm, pronunciation, and attention to stress patterns without feeling like formal study.

title: Project Notes
text: A good project note explains what was done, why it matters, and what should happen next.

title: Field Data
text: Field data becomes more useful when each observation includes location, date, method, and context.

title: Map Layers
text: Map layers help organize different types of information, such as vegetation, water, roads, and sampling points.

title: Scientific Methods
text: A clear method allows another researcher to understand, evaluate, and repeat the same procedure.

title: Code Comments
text: Useful comments explain why a decision was made, not only what the code is doing.

title: Git Commit
text: A commit should describe a meaningful change, such as adding a feature, fixing a bug, or improving documentation.

title: Coastal Erosion
text: Coastal erosion happens when waves, currents, storms, or human activities remove sediment from the shoreline.

title: Mangrove Protection
text: Mangroves reduce wave energy, trap sediment, store carbon, and provide habitat for many organisms.

title: Water Quality
text: Water quality can be described using variables such as temperature, salinity, turbidity, and dissolved oxygen.

title: Drone Mapping
text: Drone mapping can produce detailed images that support coastal monitoring, habitat classification, and field planning.

title: Spatial Patterns
text: Spatial patterns help reveal where environmental changes are concentrated and which areas need more attention.

title: Database Records
text: Each database record should represent one clear observation, event, sample, or object.

title: Vocabulary Review
text: Vocabulary becomes stronger when new words are connected to real tasks, images, sounds, and decisions.

title: Pronunciation Practice
text: Pronunciation improves when you listen carefully, repeat slowly, and compare your speech with a clear model.

title: Song Study
text: A song study can focus on vocabulary, rhythm, stress, intonation, and useful expressions.

title: Academic Writing
text: Academic writing values clarity, evidence, structure, and careful explanation of methods and results.

title: Research Question
text: A strong research question is specific, answerable, and connected to a real problem.

title: Data Cleaning
text: Data cleaning removes errors, standardizes values, and prepares information for analysis.

title: Coordinates
text: Coordinates identify locations on Earth and allow field data to be displayed on a map.

title: Remote Sensing
text: Remote sensing collects information from satellites, aircraft, or drones without direct contact with the target.

title: Image Classification
text: Image classification assigns pixels or objects to categories such as vegetation, water, sand, or urban area.

title: Species Identification
text: Species identification requires attention to shape, color, habitat, size, and reliable taxonomic references.

title: Ecological Resilience
text: Ecological resilience describes how well an ecosystem can recover after stress or disturbance.

title: Scientific Evidence
text: Scientific evidence supports conclusions through observations, measurements, comparisons, and transparent methods.

title: Literature Search
text: A literature search helps you understand what is already known and where new work can contribute.

title: Abstract First
text: Reading the abstract first helps you decide whether the full paper is relevant to your project.

title: Figure Caption
text: A figure caption should describe what the reader sees and why the figure is important.

title: Presentation Skills
text: A clear presentation uses simple language, strong visuals, and a logical sequence of ideas.

title: Listening Strategy
text: When listening in English, focus first on the main idea, then listen again for details.

title: Technical Vocabulary
text: Technical vocabulary is easier to remember when it appears in your own maps, scripts, papers, and notes.

title: Workflow Design
text: A good workflow reduces repeated effort and makes project steps easier to check and improve.

title: Version Control
text: Version control allows you to experiment with changes while keeping a history of previous work.

title: Static Website
text: A static website can be fast, simple, and reliable when the content does not require a private server.

title: API Integration
text: An API integration lets one application send data to another service in a controlled way.

title: Notion Backend
text: A Notion backend can store vocabulary, notes, and ideas while keeping sensitive tokens outside the public website.

title: Vercel Function
text: A Vercel function can receive data from the browser and safely forward it to an external service.

title: Fallback Storage
text: Fallback storage keeps user data locally when the online service is unavailable.

title: Learning Habit
text: A small learning habit works best when it is connected to tasks you already care about.

title: Ocean Current
text: An ocean current moves water, heat, nutrients, and organisms across coastal and offshore environments.

title: Sediment Transport
text: Sediment transport explains how sand and mud move along beaches, rivers, and estuaries.

title: Benthic Habitat
text: A benthic habitat includes the bottom surface and the organisms living on or near it.

title: Musical Intonation
text: Musical intonation and spoken intonation both depend on careful listening to pitch movement.
